title: Apple cider vinegar hair rinse
subtitle: A dilute apple cider vinegar rinse with lavender and rosemary to smooth the hair cuticle
excerpt: Apple cider vinegar diluted with water makes a fast, inexpensive hair rinse that smooths the hair cuticle and helps remove product build-up. The ratio is 1 part ACV to 4 parts water. Lavender and rosemary essential oils offset the vinegar smell and rinse out cleanly. The vinegar scent is not detectable once the hair dries.
sourceNotes: Apple cider vinegar hair rinse: traditional natural hair care. ACV dilution 1:4 vinegar to water; lower dilution (1:3) for oily hair, higher (1:5 to 1:8) for colour-treated or sensitive scalp. Raw unfiltered ACV preferred. Essential oils at 0.5 to 1% for light scent masking; rinse out and do not remain on scalp. pH approximately 3.5 to 4.5 after dilution.

recipe.batchNotes: Make up to 500 ml at a time. Use within 2 weeks.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Can be made ahead and stored in a spray bottle. Shake before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 weeks at room temperature, or 4 weeks refrigerated. No preservative; acidity alone is not sufficient for long-term preservation.

glossaryTerms.definition: The outermost layer of the hair shaft, made of overlapping scales. Alkaline products (such as shampoo) lift the cuticle; acidic rinses such as this one smooth it back down, reducing frizz and improving shine.

Apple cider vinegar must be diluted before applying to hair. Undiluted ACV (pH 2.5 to 3) is too acidic and can irritate the scalp and weaken hair over time. A 1:4 dilution gives a working pH of 3.5 to 4.5.

to clean. An acidic rinse after shampooing closes the cuticle back down. Apple cider vinegar at the right

achieves this without stripping the hair. The vinegar smell dissipates as the hair dries; adding lavender and rosemary essential oils helps during the rinse itself. Use raw unfiltered ACV for best results.

items.prepNote: 4 parts water; use cool or lukewarm, not hot

content.text: Combine the apple cider vinegar and water in a spray bottle or measuring jug. If using essential oils, add them and shake briefly to disperse (they will not fully dissolve in water; shake before each use).

After shampooing and conditioning, pour or spray the rinse over the hair, working through from roots to ends. Leave for 1 to 2 minutes, then rinse thoroughly with cool water. Do not leave on the scalp undiluted or without rinsing.
2 weeks at room temperature, 4 weeks refrigerated. Shake before each use if essential oils have been added.
